criteria: domain_mismatch: NLI contradiction >80% on Equal/Superset (critical) or >50% (moderate)
vocabulary_inflation: bi-encoder/STS gap >0.15 with STS <0.60 (moderate) or >0.12 (low)

critical_elements.title: Deployer workforce AI literacy
critical_elements.issue: Matched to Risk Management KSA but obligation is about workforce AI literacy for deployers. Functional domain is LS/AG, not RM.

critical_elements.title: Data and data governance — representative data
critical_elements.issue: Matched to AG governance skill but obligation is about ensuring training data representativeness. Functional domain is DQ, not AG.

critical_elements.title: Fundamental rights impact assessment — content
critical_elements.issue: Matched to Security/Privacy knowledge but obligation is about fundamental rights impact assessment methodology. May need RC or AG domain KSA.

critical_elements.title: GPAI systemic risk — adversarial testing
critical_elements.issue: Matched to AG governance skill but obligation is about adversarial testing (red-teaming) for GPAI models. Functional domain is AI/SP, not AG.

critical_elements.title: Fundamental rights impact assessment — obligation
critical_elements.issue: Matched to AG governance skill but obligation is about conducting fundamental rights impact assessments. Overlaps with AG but may need dedicated RC/AG KSA for rights-based AI assessment.

critical_elements.title: Deployer input data relevance
critical_elements.issue: Matched to Data Governance task but obligation is about ensuring input data relevance and representativeness for deployed AI. May need DQ-domain KSA for operational data quality in AI deployment.

critical_elements.title: Record-keeping — automatic logging
critical_elements.issue: Matched to Security/Privacy task but obligation is about designing automatic logging capabilities for AI systems. Functional domain is AI/TF, not SP.

critical_elements.title: Accuracy, robustness and cybersecurity
critical_elements.issue: Matched to AG governance framework knowledge but obligation requires AI quality engineering — accuracy measurement, robustness testing, cybersecurity integration. Functional domain is AI/SP, not AG. NLI contradiction 95.6% is the clearest domain mismatch signal in this STRM.

critical_elements.title: Post-market monitoring
critical_elements.issue: Classified Equal at STS 0.729 but NLI contradiction 90.6%. Post-market monitoring for AI systems may require operational monitoring KSAs beyond governance skill.

critical_elements.title: Serious incident reporting details
critical_elements.issue: Classified Equal but NLI contradiction 86.1%. Incident reporting may need dedicated RC-domain KSA for AI incident response and regulatory notification procedures.

critical_elements.title: Record-keeping — traceability
critical_elements.issue: Classified Equal but NLI contradiction 85.4%. AI traceability and audit trail design may require TF or AI domain KSA rather than AG governance skill.

analysis.summary: 24 of 62 elements (38.7%) flagged with gap signals. 11 critical, 8 moderate, 5 low. The initial assessment of zero gaps was incorrect — it was an artifact of missing gap detection logic in the scoring pipeline.
analysis.correction_note: The original gap register (2026-04-01) reported zero gap signals because the scoring pipeline hardcoded gap_signal to null for all elements. Retroactive gap detection using multi-method signal analysis revealed 24 flagged elements, 11 of which are critical domain mismatches.

pattern_analysis.ag_gravity_well: The most consistent pattern is AG-domain KSAs being matched to obligations from other functional domains. AG vocabulary (governance, framework, oversight, assessment) shares surface-level tokens with regulatory language from any domain, creating a gravity well in candidate selection. 5 of 11 critical elements matched to AG-S-005 alone.
pattern_analysis.candidate_selection_bias: The bi-encoder candidate identification step (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) preferentially surfaces AG KSAs for regulatory text because governance language is shared vocabulary. Top-5 candidates may systematically exclude functionally correct KSAs from DQ, AI, TF, and SP domains.
pattern_analysis.regulatory_vocabulary_gap: Legal prose shares vocabulary patterns with governance/compliance KSAs, inflating similarity scores for AG and RC domains while suppressing scores for technically specific domains (AI quality engineering, data quality operations, adversarial testing).

strongest_domain_coverage.AG: AI Governance — highest representation but over-represented due to vocabulary overlap. Legitimately covers AI literacy (Art. 4), human oversight (Art. 14), quality management (Art. 17).
strongest_domain_coverage.RC: Regulatory Compliance — covers provider/deployer obligations (Art. 16, 26), conformity assessment (Art. 34, 43).
strongest_domain_coverage.RM: Risk Management — covers risk management system (Art. 9), technical documentation (Art. 53).

gap_themes.description: Accuracy measurement, robustness testing, cybersecurity integration for AI systems. No WIDAI KSA directly addresses the engineering discipline of building AI systems to quality standards.
Structured adversarial testing of GPAI models for systemic risk identification. Distinct from general security testing — requires AI-specific attack methodology.
Conducting and documenting fundamental rights impact assessments for high-risk AI. Requires intersection of legal/ethical analysis with technical AI assessment.
Designing automatic logging, audit trails, and traceability mechanisms for AI systems. Technical engineering competency, not governance.

gap_themes.theme: AI Training Data Quality and Representativeness
gap_themes.description: Ensuring training data representativeness and input data relevance. Data quality discipline applied to AI-specific contexts.

gap_themes.theme: AI Incident Response and Regulatory Notification
gap_themes.description: Post-market monitoring, serious incident identification, and regulatory notification procedures for AI systems.

analysis.cross_strm_note: Several gap themes corroborate findings from previous STRMs: AI incident response (NICE-GAP-002, DCWF-GAP-001), supply chain integrity for AI (NICE-GAP-005, DCWF-GAP-003). The fundamental rights impact assessment theme is novel to the EU AI Act STRM. Cross-STRM corroboration strengthens the case for KSA creation during Phase 1D synthesis.
